Object detectors using CNNs have been developing rapidly.
Many 3D object detectors have been studied in recent years.

CNNs were built using feature-centric voting in the method Vote3Deep for detecting objects in point clouds.
RPN was used to learn objectness from geometric shapes in the task of amodal 3D object detection in RGB-D images.
RPN was used along with joint object recognition network to extract geometric features in 3D and color features in 2D for object detection.
3D fully convolutional network was applied to point cloud data for vehicle detection.
VoxelNet designed an end-to-end trainable detector based on learning-based voxelization using fully connected layers.
HVNet fused the multi-scale voxel feature encoder at the point-wise level for solving various sizes of the feature map.
PIXOR converted the point cloud to a 3D BEV map for real-time 3D object detection with an RPN-free single-stage based model.
PointNet was utilized to learn the representation of point clouds in the PointPillars (PP)-based method.

Recent method utilized PointPillars for achieving fast 3D object detection.
CenterPoint employs the backbone architecture using PointPillars for achieving good performance in the wild situation.
CenterPoint was introduced as an anchor-free detector that predicted the center of an object using PointPillars or VoxelNet-based feature encoder.

The KD method transfers knowledge to produce a lightweight point cloud detector by involving interchange transfer and learning coarse knowledge by increasing the similarity of compressed features and fine knowledge by decompressing map-view features using the channel-wise autoencoder.

The proposed method in the context introduces a method to guide multiple detection heads using head relation-aware self-attention, which refines knowledge by considering the relation of instances and properties.
Compared to lightweight network-based methods, the student networks are able to derive stable performance with fewer parameters and FLOPS in 3D object detection.
Relation studies demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm.
Extensive experiments verify the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm on large-scale datasets.
The proposed method achieves competitive performance against state-of-the-art methods.
